observations.recommendation:
1.16 We recommended and the Local Executive Head agreed to:
a. Require the Inventory Committee to continue the conduct of
complete physical count on all types of property, plant and
equipment, and prepare the prescribed annual RPCPPE thereon
which shall be submitted to the Municipal Accountant for
reconciliation of records, and adjust the books accordingly;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the GSO to
update the affected records i.e., PCs and PPELCs; and
c. In case of unreconciled PPE balances, ensure full implementation
of “one-time cleansing of PPE account balances” as provided for
in COA Circular No. 2020-006.

2.11 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the
Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to:
a. Continue the conduct of complete physical count, on a semestral
basis, for all types of inventories and prepare the RPCI as of June
30 and as of December 31 in accordance with the prescribed
format;
b. Update the entries in the Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards
from time to time; and
c. Exert more efforts in reconciling the balances per book and per
count in order to present fairly the account balance in the
Financial Statement.

3.10 We reiterated our previous recommendation and the Local Chief
Executive agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the
preparation and submission of the monthly BRS within the prescribed
period to the Audit Team for review and evaluation. In case of delayed
production of the bank statements, securing a snap-shots as of a given
date will do.

4.6 We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipality
ensure that copies of contract documents (such as MOA, purchase order and supporting documents) are submitted for contract review
within the prescribed five (5) day period from the execution / issuance
of the contract.

1. We recommended and Management agreed to require the Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical count of all types of inventories and prepare the prescribed RPCI every six (6) months to be submitted to the Accounting Office for reconciliation with the subsidiary accounts and balances to ensure reliability of inventory accounts.

In view of the foregoing deficiencies, we strongly recommended and Management agreed to instruct the following:
2. Immediate submission of all unsubmitted reports to the Office of Auditor such as the RCI duly certified by the Municipal Treasurer, ROD, DVs, payrolls, together with their supporting documents;
3. Initiate the keeping of separate updated subsidiary records for each transfer, whether or not maintained in separate bank accounts;
4. Make the necessary adjustments and establish of the necessary accounts, to correct the ledgers, trial balance, and the financial statements; and
5. Finally, submit to the Auditor’s Office a schedule of all the transfers/subisidies actually received by the Management including their bank accounts, if any.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipality ensure that copies of contract documents (such as MOA, purchase order and supporting documents) are submitted for contract review within the prescribed five (5) day period from the execution / issuance of the contract.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the preparation and prompt submission of the monthly BRS to the Audit Team for their review pursuant to Section 74 PD No. 1445 and Section 3 of COA Circular No. 96-011. In case of delayed production of the bank statements, securing a snap-shots as of a given date will do.

8. Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit separate RODs for each cash advances, together with all the necessary supporting liquidation papers, to the Municipal Accountant immediately after the purpose has been served; and

9. Require all accountable officers to faithfully submit their monthly mandatory reports such as the RCIs and RAAFs together with the necessary attachments within the time frame prescribed by the Commission.

10. We recommended and Management agreed to require the GAD Focal to establish and maintain a GAD database containing Sex-Disaggregated data and gender statistics and consider such in the preparation of the GAD Plan and Budget pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 in order to gather and address all pressing gender issues within the Municipality
11. Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Accountant and the General Services Office with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

12. We recommended that Management require the Property Officer to prepare and submit the Property Insurance Form (PIF) for CY 2018 to the provisions COA Circular No. 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018, and submitted to the Auditor for our review and evaluation.
13. We recommended that Management create its own Municipal Solid Waste Management Board in compliance with the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.

1.8 We recommended and Management agreed to require the Inventory
Committee to conduct complete physical count of all types of
inventories and prepare the prescribed RPCI every six (6) months to
be submitted to the Accounting Office for reconciliation with the
subsidiary accounts and balances to ensure reliability of inventory
accounts.

2.5 In view of the foregoing deficiencies, we strongly recommended and
Management agreed to instruct the following:
a. Immediate submission of all unsubmitted reports to the Office of
Auditor such as the RCI duly certified by the Municipal
Treasurer, ROD, DVs, payrolls, together with their supporting
documents;
b. Initiate the keeping of separate updated subsidiary records for
each transfer, whether or not maintained in separate bank
accounts;
c. Make the necessary adjustments and establish of the necessary
accounts, to correct the ledgers, trial balance, and the financial
statements; and
d. Finally, submit to the Auditor’s Office a schedule of all the
transfers/subisidies actually received by the Management
including their bank accounts, if any.

3.4 We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant to facilitate the preparation and prompt submission of the
monthly BRS to the Audit Team for their review pursuant to Section
74 PD No. 1445 and Section 3 of COA Circular No. 96-011. In case of
delayed production of the bank statements, securing a snap-shots as of
a given date will do.

4.11 We recommended and Management agreed to:
a) Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit separate RODs for
each cash advances, together with all the necessary supporting
liquidation papers, to the Municipal Accountant immediately after
the purpose has been served; and
b) Require all accountable officers to faithfully submit their monthly
mandatory reports such as the RCIs and RAAFs together with the
necessary attachments within the time frame prescribed by the
Commission.

5.5 We recommended and Management agreed to require the GAD Focal
to establish and maintain a GAD database containing Sex-
Disaggregated data and gender statistics and consider such in the
preparation of the GAD Plan and Budget pursuant to PCW-DILG-
DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 in order to gather and address all
pressing gender issues within the Municipality.
1.Create the Inventory Committee of the Municipality to conduct the inventory on the physical count of its road network and prepare a report thereon;
2.Require the Municipal Accountant to update the local roads network accounts based on the result of the physical count; and
3.Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Accountant and the General Services Office with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

4.We recommended that the Management create a separate special account in the General Fund (SAGF) fund for the BGCM Fund through an ordinance by the local sangguniang pursuant to Section 313 of RA No. 7160 for purposes of easy monitoring/tracking of the utilization of the fund.
5.A monthly report on BGCM fund utilization and status of implementation of PPAs pursuant to DBM Local Budget Circular No. 125;

6.A quarterly Fund Receipt and Utilization Report (FRUR) for BGCM Fund as special account for General Fund and a year-end FRUR, which contains the summary of transactions for the year as integral part of the schedules of the General Fund trial balance for purposes of proper recording in the books of accounts
7.We recommended that the Municipal Accountant attach the corresponding JEV, numbered and properly filled-out, on every disbursement voucher and payroll paid.

8.We therefore reiterate our previous recommendation that Management strictly submit to the Office of the Auditor copies of the contract/purchase order including all pertinent supporting documents thereto as mandated in COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 within five (5) days after its perfection and/or approval.

9.We therefore reiterate our previous recommendation that Management require the GAD Focal Person to prepare the GAD Plan and Budget at the beginning of the year and the GAD Accomplishment Report after the yearend, thereafter submit said plan and report pursuant to item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 for the timely audit verification and review thereof.

10.Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit a separate ROD for each of the various cash advances together with all the necessary supporting liquidating instruments to the Municipal Accountant immediately after the purpose has been served;

11.Require all accountable officers to faithfully submit their monthly mandatory reports such as the RCIs and RAAFs together with the necessary attachments within the time frame prescribed by the Commission.

12.We recommended that Management require the Property Officer to prepare and submit the Property Insurance Form (PIF) for CY 2018 to the provisions COA Circular No. 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018, and submitted to the Auditor for our review and evaluation.

13.We recommended that Management comply with the submission to the Auditor’s Office copies of Purchase Orders (POs)/ Contracts and Acceptance and Inspection Reports (AIRs) relative to the execution thereof and within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of goods, respectively, pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 95-006, which precluded the Audit.
14.We recommended that Management create its own Municipal Solid Waste Management Board in compliance with the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.

5. We recommended that Management comply with the submission to the Auditor’s Office copies of Purchase Orders (POs)/Contracts and Acceptance and Inspection Reports (AIRs) relative to the procurement of goods within five (5) working days from the execution thereof and within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of goods, respectively, pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 95-006.

6. We recommended that Management require the Municipal Planning and Development officer to prepare the GAD Plan and Budget at the beginning of the year and the GAD Accomplishment Report after the yearend, thereafter submit said plan and report pursuant to item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 for the timely audit verification and review thereof.

1.6 We recommended that the Honorable Mayor:
a) Create an Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count
of its road network and prepare a report thereon;
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to update the local roads
network accounts based on the result of the physical count; and
c) Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Accountant
and the General Services Officer with the complete description
and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

2.6 We recommended that the Management create a separate special
account in the General Fund (SAGF) for the BGCM Fund through an
ordinance by the local sangguniang pursuant to Section 313 of RA No.
7160 for purposes of easy monitoring/tracking of the utilization of the
fund.

3.6 We recommended that the Management prepare:
a) A monthly report on BGCM fund utilization and status of
implementation of PPAs pursuant to DBM Local Budget Circular
No. 125; and
b) A quarterly Fund Receipt and Utilization Report (FRUR) for
BGCM Fund as special account for General Fund and a year-end
FRUR, which contains the summary of transactions for the year
as integral part of the schedules of the General Fund trial balance
for purposes of proper recording in the books of accounts.

4.5 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant attach the
corresponding JEV, numbered and properly filled-out, on every
disbursement voucher and payroll paid.

5.5 We therefore reiterated our previous recommendation that
Management strictly submit to the Office of the Auditor copies of the
contract/purchase order including all pertinent supporting documents
thereto as mandated in COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12,
2009 within five (5) days after its perfection and/or approval.

6.4 We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Person to
prepare the GAD Plan and Budget at the beginning of the year and
the GAD Accomplishment Report after the yearend, thereafter
submit said plan and report pursuant to item V of COA Circular No.
2014-001 for the timely audit verification and review thereof.

7.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit a separate ROD for
each of the various cash advances together with all the necessary
supporting liquidating instruments to the Municipal Accountant
immediately after the purpose has been served; and
b) Require all accountable officers to faithfully submit their monthly
mandatory reports such as the RCIs and RAAFs together with the
necessary attachments within the time frame prescribed by the
Commission.
1. Create an Inventory Committee of at least three (3) or more responsible officials to include always the Property Officer and a representative from Accounting Office;
2. Instruct and supervise the conduct of physical count of all properties of the Municipality with a witness of a representative from the Office of the Auditor;
3. Require the Municipal Treasurer to maintain property ledger cards and reconcile with the report of the Municipal Accountant; and
4. Prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) duly signed by the committee members to the Office of the Auditor.
7. We recommended that Management create its own Municipal Solid Waste Management Board in compliance with the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.

8. We recommend that Management require the Property Officer to prepare and submit the Property Insurance Form (PIF) for CY 2018 to the provisions COA Circular No. 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018, and submitted to the Auditor for our review and evaluation.

We recommended that Management:
a. Form its own Municipal Solid Waste Management Board; and
b. Comply with all applicable rules and regulations of Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 to ensure protection of public health and environment.

We recommended that Management require the Property Officer to prepare and submit the Property Insurance Form (PIF) for CY 2018 to the provisions COA Circular No. 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018, and submitted to the Auditor for our review and evaluation.

1.6 We reiterated to recommend that Management:
a. Create an Inventory Committee of at least three (3) or more
responsible officials to include always the Property Officer and a
representative from Accounting Office;
b. Instruct and supervise the conduct of physical inventory count of
all properties of the Municipality with a witness of a representative
from the Office of the Auditor;
c. Require the Municipal Treasurer to maintain property ledger cards
and reconcile with the report of the Municipal Accountant; and
d. Prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property,
Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) duly signed by the committee
members to the Office of the Auditor.

2.4 We recommended that Management comply with the submission to the
Auditor’s Office copies of Purchase Orders(POs)/Contracts and
Acceptance and Inspection Reports (AIRs) relative to the procurement
of goods within five (5) working days from the execution thereof and
within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of goods, respectively,
pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 95-006.

3.4 We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Person to
prepare the GAD Plan and Budget at the beginning of the year and the
GAD Accomplishment Report after the yearend, thereafter submit said
plan and report pursuant to item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 for the
timely audit verification and review thereof.

4.4 We recommended that Management create its own Municipal Solid
Waste Management Board in compliance with the Ecological Solid
Waste Management Act of 2000.

5.6 We recommended that Management require the Property Officer to
prepare the Property Insurance Form (PIF) for CY 2019 in pursuance to
the provisions of COA Circular No. 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018 and
submit thereafter to the Office of Auditor for auditorial review and
evaluation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Accountant to secure the monthly bank statement of account from its authorized government depository bank (AGDB) for all their bank accounts maintained and then prepare the corresponding monthly bank reconciliation statements for each account in accordance with Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and Sections 3.1 and 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011.

We recommended that Management:
a. Direct the Municipal Engineer or the MPDC, to submit the complete list of all PPAs in CY 2018;
b. Provide the Audit Team with updated information as to the status of the publicity of PPAs; and
c. Strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommended that Management require the MDRRMO in coordination with the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit to the Auditor’s Office the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF, as well as the periodic reports required Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and COA Circular No. 2014-002.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Planning and Development officer to prepare the GAD Plan and Budget at the beginning of the year and the GAD Accomplishment Report after the yearend, and submit said plan and report pursuant to item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 for the timely verification and audit.

We recommended that the Accounting Office and Treasury Office continue to be compliant in the deduction of GSIS, Philhealth and Pag-ibig premium contributions and Loan Amortizations and the remittance thereof including the amorizations from employees’ compensation and in the remittance thereof together with the government share within the prescribed period.

1.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Accountant
to secure the monthly bank statement from its authorized government
depository bank (AGDB) for all their bank accounts maintained and then
prepare the corresponding monthly bank reconciliation statements for
each bank account in accordance with Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and
Sections 3.1 and 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011.

2.6 We recommended that Management:
a. Direct the Municipal Engineer or the MPDC, to submit the complete
list of all PPAs in CY 2018;
b. Provide the Audit Team with updated information as to the status of
the publicity of PPAs; and
c. Strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January
30, 2013.

3.11 We recommended that Management require the MDRRMO in
coordination with the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit to the
Auditor’s Office the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the
LDRRMF, as well as the periodic reports required Item 5.1.5 of COA
Circular No. 2012-002 and COA Circular No. 2014-002.

4.9 We recommended that Management
a.) form its own Municipal Solid Waste Management Board; and
b.) comply with all applicable rules and regulations of Ecological Solid
Waste Management Act of 2000 to ensure protection of public health
and environment.

5.6 We recommended that Management require the Property Officer to
prepare and submit the Property Insurance Form (PIF) for CY 2018 to
the provisions COA Circular No. 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018, and
submitted to the Auditor for our review and evaluation.

6.4 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Planning and
Development officer to prepare the GAD Plan and Budget at the
beginning of the year and the GAD Accomplishment Report after the
yearend, and submit said plan and report pursuant to item V of COA
Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 for the timely verification
and audit.

7.6 We recommended that the Accounting Office and Treasury Office
continue to be compliant in the deduction of GSIS, Philhealth and Pag-
ibig Fund premium contributions and Loan Amortizations and the
remittance thereof including the Government share within the
prescribed period.

8.7 We recommended that responsible persons should settle their audit
suspensions in the sum of ₱4,035,562.96 as soon as possible. Further,
Management should revisit existing disbursement controls of the LGU
and install appropriate measures designed to safeguard public funds
from possible misuse.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the BRS and its supporting documents monthly to the Auditor, within ten days after receipt of the Bank Statements in compliance with the Section 74 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 96-011 dated October 2,1996.

We recommended that Management create the Inventory Committee composed of at least three (3) officials to conduct complete physical count of all properties owned by the Municipality and prepare a report thereon in accordance with Section 124 and 114of NGAS Manual for LGUs. We also recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE), respectively in compliance to Section 114 of same Manual.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strictly observe the issuance of official receipt to each payor as proof of acknowledgement of receipt of payment and ensure that the Report of Collection and Deposits is prepared showing the details of payors and the ORs in compliance with the Section 68 paragraph 1 of PD 1445. Thereafter, the said RCDs should be submitted to the Auditor regularly.

We encouraged and urged the Local Chief Executive to establish a sound ecological solid waste management systems and constitute the Municipality Solid Waste Management (SWM) Boards in pursuant to Section 2 (par. 2) Rule VIII and Section 1, Rule VI of IRR of RA 5447.
observations.managementAction: Directed the TWG thru the MPDC to work the backlog.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly implement the mandatory deductions from its personnel pertaining to GSIS, Pag-ibig, Philhealth and withholding tax and remitting the same on time in compliance with the above-cited provisions of laws and regulations.

We recommended that Management widen the allocation of development fund to utilization of 20% to the three priority programs and projects such as social development, economic development and environment management.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to prepare and furnish the Auditor a copy of the Accomplishment Report for GAD showing the programs, projects and activities undertaken during the year.
observations.managementAction: Submitted and Furnished copy of GAD report

observations.recommendation: a. We recommended that Management strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 date January 30, 2013 to enhance transparency on its operation and project implementation; b. We also recommended that Management furnish the Audit Team the necessary information within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/project/activities and post signboard and/or public notices in the project site for the timely validation.
observations.managementAction: Submitted and Furnished copy of PPAs and BID Awards

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be directed to prepare the required Bank Reconciliation Statements to come up with a reliable balance of Cash in Bank account in compliance with COA Circular No. 976-011 dated October 2, 1996 and Section 74 of P.D. 1445.

It is recommended that the management should widen the allocation of utilization of 20% development fund to the three priority programs and projects such as social development, economic development and environment management.

Encourage and urge the Local Chief Executive to establish a sound ecological solid waste management system and constitute the Municipality Solid Waste Management (SWM) Boards in pursuant to Section 2 (par. 2), Rule VIII and Section 1, Rule VI of IRR of RA 5447.

It is recommended that the LGU should include in its annual budget an appropriation for LGU leagues contributions as a source of fund of the leagues for its continue existence and for carrying out its powers and functions as well as programs/ activities effectively and efficiently. In case there is a disbursement by LGU for such contributions, the LGU should ask for an Official Receipt as proof of receipt of payment of contributions to the league. In the event that there is fund transfer to the league, a liquidation thereof must be demanded by the concerned LGU to the league.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the BRS and its supporting documents monthly to the Auditor, within ten days after receipt of the Bank Statements in compliance with the Section 74 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 96-011 dated October 2,1996.

We recommended that Management create the Inventory Committee composed of at least three (3) officials to conduct complete physical count of all properties owned by the Municipality and prepare a report thereon in accordance with Section 124 and 114of NGAS Manual for LGUs. .
We also recommended that the LCE direct Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE), respectively in compliance to Section 114 of same Manual.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer  strictly observe the issuance of official receipt to each payor as proof of acknowledgement of receipt of payment and ensure that the  Report of Collection and Deposits is prepared showing the details of payors and the ORs in compliance with the Section 68 paragraph 1 of PD 1445. Thereafter, the said RCDs should be submitted to the Auditor regularly.

We encouraged and urged the Local Chief Executive to establish a sound ecological solid waste management systems and constitute the Municipality Solid Waste Management (SWM) Boards in pursuant to Section 2 (par. 2) Rule VIII and Section 1, Rule VI of IRR of RA 5447.

We recommended that Management strictly implement the mandatory deductions pertaining to GSIS, Pag-ibig, Philhealth and  withholding tax for personnel and remitting the same on time on time in compliance with the above-cited provisions of laws and regulations.

We recommended that the Management widen the allocation of utilization of 20% development fund to the three priority programs and projects such as social development, economic development and environment management.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to prepare and furnish the Auditor a copy of the Accomplishment Report for GAD showing the programs, projects and activities undertaken during the year.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the provisions of the COA Circular No. 2013-004 date January 30, 2013 to enhance transparency on its operation and project implementation;
We also recommended that Management furnish the Audit Team the necessary information within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/project/activities and post signboard and/or public notices in the project site for the timely validation.

It is recommended that the top management should initiate necessary reforms for fiscal reporting and accountability by:
demonstrating leadership by example;
training personnel to gain work competence and/or hiring additional qualified personnel and or restructuring existing personnel giving the right job for the right person; and
Enforcing due compliance with laws, rules and regulations on the rendition of financial reports.

It is further recommended that top management should direct the concerned municipal accountable officer to submit promptly the period financial reports together with the pertinent supporting documents to the concerned local auditor so that timely post-audit of transactions could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated. Consequently, the top management could also be informed immediately of any deficiencies/errors detected and corrections /adjustments thereof could be effected to their advantage.

The Implementing Agency (IA) to which the funds are transferred for the purpose of prosecuting/implementing the project should faithfully perform the duties and responsibilities enumerated in Item 6 of COA Circular No. 94-013.

We recommend that the management should furnish COA copy of any contract/ purchase order including all pertinent supporting documents as enumerated under COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12,2009 within (5) days  after perfection/ approval/ execution.

We recommend that management should conduct a thorough inquiry on the circumstances surrounding the existence of those dormant accounts, and their proper disposition as prescribed under existing regulations.
observations.reason: The alleged transactions takes place during the past administration and the present administration did not know where to traceback.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer be directed to furnish the Auditor a copy of the Accomplishment Report for GAD showing the programs, projects and activities undertaken during the year.
observations.managementAction: Officials concerned are instructed to comply with the rule and regulations in handling GAD
observations.reason: The mgt. failed to completely adhere to the Implementation of GAD..

observations.managementAction: Officials concerned tried their best to reconcile with the GSIS..
observations.reason: Records of the Past Administration could be hardly traced.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be directed to prepare the required Bank Reconciliation Statements to come up with a reliable balance of Cash in Bank account in compliance with COA Circular No. 976-011 dated October 2, 1996 and Section 74 of P.D. 1445.

It is recommended that the top management should initiate necessary reforms for fiscal reporting and accountability by:
	demonstrating leadership by example;
	training personnel to gain work competence and/or hiring additional qualified personnel and or restructuring existing personnel giving the right job for the right person; and
	Enforcing due compliance with laws, rules and regulations on the rendition of financial reports.

It is further recommended that top management should direct the concerned municipal accountable officer to submit promptly the period financial reports together with the pertinent supporting documents to the concerned local auditor so that timely post-audit of transactions could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated. Consequently, the top management could also be informed immediately of any deficiencies/errors detected and corrections /adjustments thereof could be effected to their advantage.

It is recommended that the management should widen the allocation of utilization of 20% development fund to the three priority programs and projects such as social development, economic development and environment management.

The Implementing Agency (IA) to which the funds are transferred for the purpose of prosecuting/implementing the project should faithfully perform the duties and responsibilities enumerated in Item 6 of COA Circular No. 94-013.

Encourage and urge the Local Chief Executive to establish a sound ecological solid waste management systems and constitute the Municipality Solid Waste Management (SWM) Boards in pursuant to Section 2 (par. 2), Rule VIII and Section 1, Rule VI of IRR of RA 5447.

It is recommended that the LGU should include in its annual budget an appropriation for LGU leagues contributions as a source of fund of the leagues for its continue existence and for carrying out its powers and functions as well as programs/ activities effectively and efficiently. In case there is a disbursement by LGU for such contributions, the LGU should ask for an Official Receipt as proof of receipt of payment of contributions to the league. In the event that there is fund transfer to the league, a liquidation thereof must be demanded by the concerned LGU to the league.

The local chief executive of the municipality should:
create an Inventory Committee: which composed of the representative of the Chief Executive , the Treasurer and Property or Supply Officer or Accountable Officer as members; and whose functions are: (1) to conduct a physical inventory of all equipment belonging to their respective offices at least once a year, to be witnessed by representative of the agency auditor; and (2) to submit  an Annual Physical Inventory of all property under the agency control as of 31, December, General Form (41) not later than 31 January of each year; and 

direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain a subsidiary records particularly Property Ledger Cards (PLC) for property, plant and equipment accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Property Officer are directed to initiate the conduct of physical inventory of PPE within the target period.
observations.reason: Still in the process of updating records.

observations.recommendation: Required the Municipal Treasurer to strictly observe the issuance of official receipt to each payor as proof of acknowledgement of receipt of payment and the preparation of report of collection thereof that would show the details of payors in compliance with the Section 68 paragraph 1 of PD 1445 and Sections 16, 22 and 23 of NGAS, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The concern official promised to start issuing ORs as a proof of acknowledgement of receipts..
observations.reason: Some collections were not issued with corresponding ORs maybe because it was forgotten.

observations.recommendation:
Cause for the proper orientation of concerned personnel on accounting                   and reporting of LDRRMF pursuant to COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, COA Circular 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 and RA 10121 (PDRRM).  Specifically, make sure that:
 
a separate Registry for Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO) is properly maintained for LDRRMF, namely, RAAO 		for QRF, RAAO 		for MOOE and RAAO 		for CO; 
a monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF is duly rendered; and
all unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and LDRRMF-MOOE are transferred to the Special Trust Fund under the account 																																																																																																																																																																																																																																				"Trust Liability 																																																																																																																																													 (Code 438) in the Trust Funds books.
observations.managementAction: Report of LDRRMf  were updated and any unexpended balance remain intac.
observations.reason: Unexpended balance was not transfer to separate account in the Trust Fund.
